user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the rear bumper and surrounding body panels of the car, allowing for assessment of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The provided image (img_1) clearly shows significant damage, including a large dent and deformation, to the rear bumper and the area above it. This aligns with the customer's claim of a dent on the back of the car, specifically the rear bumper area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show the claimed object and part with clear damage, meeting the evidence requirements for assessing surface marks and vehicle identity.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a scratch on the front bumper. Image img_2 clearly shows significant damage, including scratches and scuff marks, on the front bumper. Image img_1 shows the front of the car but does not show this damage.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images clearly show the windshield of a car, and one of the images displays a significant crack.
claim_status_justification: The customer claimed a crack on the windshield. Image img_1 clearly shows a large crack originating from a central impact point on the windshield. Image img_2 shows the windshield but does not clearly depict the damage.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object part and the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows that the side mirror glass is shattered, which aligns with the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the rear bumper of a car, and at least one image provides a view suitable for assessing damage.
claim_status_justification: The first image (img_1) clearly shows multiple dents and surface imperfections on the rear bumper of a silver car, consistent with the user's claim of bumper damage from being tapped from behind. The second image (img_2) shows the rear of the car but does not clearly depict the damage.
